name: Climb to Beratón Port from Borja — Hidden Face of Moncayo
name_original: Subida al Puerto de Beratón desde Borja — Cara Oculta del Moncayo
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Borja traverse a diverse landscape, from arid plains with rolling hills and wind turbines to fertile almond fields and vineyards. The region is situated between the rugged Iberian mountain ranges and the Ebro Valley, offering varied natural environments. Notable features include the Sierra de Moncayo, which provides a challenging backdrop for cyclists, and the Campo de Borja region, known for its garnacha grape vineyards. This area presents a mix of terrains suitable for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Borja The most popular road cycling route is Climb to Beratón Port from Borja — Hidden Face of Moncayo, a 79.7 miles (128.3 km) trail that takes 6 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Moncayo mountain range. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Bar de Moncayo – Moncayo Roundabout loop from Antigua Estacion de Ainzon, a difficult 49.6 miles (79.8 km) path. This route offers views of the Moncayo area with substantial ascents. Local road cyclists also love the Burosque Encantado – Borja loop from Borja, a 16.3 miles (26.3 km) trail leading through the whimsical Enchanted Forest of Bureta, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. Road cycling around Borja is defined by rolling hills, vineyards, and the challenging terrain of the Sierra de Moncayo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to difficult mountain climbs. The routes in Borja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 reviews. More than 120 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Borja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Borja?
A: Borja offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 10 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult rides, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Borja?
A: The spring and early summer months are particularly pleasant for road cycling in Borja, especially for routes like the Tarazonica Greenway, when the plant colors are vibrant and storks can be seen. The region's diverse landscapes, including arid plains and vineyards, offer unique scenery throughout different seasons.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Borja?
A: The road cycling routes in Borja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from rolling hills and vineyards to the challenging climbs of the Sierra de Moncayo, and the opportunity to combine physical activity with cultural exploration.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Borja?
A: Yes, Borja offers easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20153511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burosque Encantado – Borja loop from Borja&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 16.3-mile (26.3 km) trail that takes you through the whimsical Enchanted Forest of Bureta with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs near Borja?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the Sierra de Moncayo mountain range provides a demanding backdrop. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990798979/climb-to-beraton-port-from-borja-hidden-face-of-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Beratón Port from Borja — Hidden Face of Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, a 79.7-mile (128.3 km) route with significant elevation, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40327450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar de Moncayo – Moncayo Roundabout loop from Antigua Estacion de Ainzon&lt;/a&gt;, offer substantial ascents and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Borja?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Borja are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20153511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burosque Encantado – Borja loop from Borja&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40327450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar de Moncayo – Moncayo Roundabout loop from Antigua Estacion de Ainzon&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for exploration.

Q: Can I find family-friendly road cycling routes in Borja?
A: While many routes are geared towards more experienced cyclists, the region does offer options suitable for families. The Tarazonica Greenway is a pleasant cycling path, particularly enjoyable in spring and early summer, offering a gentler ride. Look for routes described as 'easy' with less elevation gain for a family-friendly experience.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or natural features can I expect on road cycling routes around Borja?
A: Road cycling routes around Borja offer diverse scenery, from arid landscapes with rolling hills and wind turbines to fertile almond fields and vineyards, especially beautiful in winter. You'll also encounter the majestic Sierra de Moncayo and the unique Burosque Enchanted Forest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31855010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Buste Viewpoint – Bar de Moncayo loop from Borja&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions I can visit while road cycling near Borja?
A: Absolutely! The region is rich in history and culture. You can cycle to the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1755663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarazona&lt;/a&gt;, visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Veruela&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the Sanctuary of Mercy, home to the famous 'Ecce Homo' painting. The Campo de Borja region is also renowned for its wineries, offering unique tasting experiences.

Q: Are there any unique or 'hidden gem' routes to explore?
A: For a truly unique experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20153511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burosque Encantado – Borja loop from Borja&lt;/a&gt;. This route leads through the whimsical Enchanted Forest of Bureta, a magical place with treehouses made from recycled natural materials, offering a memorable and less conventional cycling adventure.

Q: Are there facilities like cafes or pubs for cyclists along the routes?
A: While specific cyclist-focused cafes or pubs are not explicitly detailed for every route, the towns and villages along the routes, such as Borja and Tarazona, offer various establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments and meals. The region's reputation for wine also means many wineries might offer tasting opportunities.

Q: What are the parking options for road cyclists in Borja?
A: Borja and its surrounding towns generally offer public parking facilities. When planning your route, it's advisable to check for parking availability near your chosen starting point, especially in larger towns like Borja or Tarazona, to ensure convenient access to the trails.

Q: Is bike rental available in Borja?
A: While the guide does not specifically list bike rental services, Borja is a destination for outdoor activities. It is recommended to check with local tourism offices or sports shops in Borja or nearby larger towns like Tarazona for current bike rental options.
tips: From this point, on the outskirts of Calcena, you have a good view of the whole town with its traditional houses and its magnificent natural environment.

Located on the Hidden Face of Moncayo, Beratón offers us wonderful views as soon as we crown its port. The climb from Calcena (where the climb begins) is not very demanding but it is very entertaining.
